candidate.description: Layer that spreads incoming requests across a pool of backend servers using round-robin, least-connections, or weighted policies, with health checks to evict failing nodes.

candidates_full.full_description: Distributing incoming work across multiple servers using algorithms (round-robin, least-connections, hash-based) and health checks; central building block of horizontally scaled systems. Includes L4 vs L7 LB, sticky sessions, and reverse-proxy deployments.
Adding more servers (rather than bigger ones) to handle load; usually requires load balancing and stateless services.
